title: The Cow (The Adventures Of Seumas Beg)
content:
Cow, Cow!
                    I and thou
        Are looking at each other's eyes
        You are lying on the grass
        Eating every time I pass,
        And you do not seem to be
        Ever in perplexity:
        You are good I'm sure, and not
        Fit for nothing but the pot:
        For your bearing is so kind,
        And your quietness so wise:
                    Cow, Cow!
                    I and thou
        Are looking at each other's eyes.
meaning:
This evocative piece by James Stephens, titled "The Cow (The Adventures Of Seumas Beg)", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
